codeReview.summary: The implementation fully satisfies the feature requirements. The TaskRunner component replaces the placeholder desktop panel with a real shell prompt UI that submits through the runtime API, renders task/status/event progress, returns provider-backed answers, prevents duplicate submission during renewal, and reattaches to in-flight work across reload/new-tab. The code is well-structured with clear separation between the runtime API client (runtime.js) and the UI component (TaskRunner.svelte). All 9 Playwright tests pass, covering prompt visibility, submission, status display, result rendering, input state management, reload reattachment, event streaming, and auth gating.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library documentation at .factory/library/runtime-shell-prompt-ui.md is comprehensive and well-structured, covering components, design decisions, data attributes for testing, and future work. This provides good shared context for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/runtime-shell-prompt-ui.md covers runtime.js API, TaskRunner.svelte component, validation assertions (VAL-RUNTIME-007, VAL-CROSS-109, VAL-CROSS-111, VAL-CROSS-121), and design decisions about sessionStorage usage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly follows the 'no auth tokens in browser storage' convention by using sessionStorage only for the task handle (a server-generated UUID), not for auth tokens. This is properly documented in both code comments and the library doc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/lib/runtime.js:45-50 and .factory/library/runtime-shell-prompt-ui.md both explicitly document that auth tokens are NEVER stored in sessionStorage.

summary: The runtime-shell-prompt-ui-and-reattachment feature is fully implemented and passes review. The code correctly implements all required functionality: shell prompt UI with runtime API submission, task/status/event progress rendering, provider-backed answer display, duplicate submission prevention via active-task guard in submitTask(), and reattachment across reload/new-tab via sessionStorage. The 9 Playwright tests all pass, and the implementation aligns with the validation contract requirements (VAL-RUNTIME-007, VAL-CROSS-109, VAL-CROSS-111, VAL-CROSS-121). The worker followed the skill procedure correctly and no shared state gaps were identified.
